diagrams.title: Hermite Curve Construction
diagrams.description: Shows a cubic Hermite curve defined by two endpoints (P0, P1) and their tangent vectors (m0, m1). The curve smoothly interpolates between the endpoints while following the direction of the tangent vectors at each end.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental definition of a Hermite curve: how four parameters (two points and two tangents) define a smooth cubic curve

diagrams.title: Hermite to Bezier Conversion
diagrams.description: Illustrates the mathematical relationship between Hermite and Bezier representations. Shows how Hermite endpoints and tangents convert to Bezier control points using the formula: B1 = P0 + m0/3 and B2 = P1 - m1/3
elements: Hermite tangents m0, m1 (shown as arrows)
Bezier control points B0, B1, B2, B3
Bezier control point handles connecting to curve
The resulting Bezier curve (identical to Hermite)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the equivalence between Hermite and Bezier formulations, showing they produce the same curve with different parameterizations

diagrams.title: Hermite Basis Functions
diagrams.description: Displays the four cubic Hermite basis/blending functions (h00, h10, h01, h11) as separate curves. These functions determine how each control parameter (P0, m0, P1, m1) contributes to the final curve at any parameter t
diagrams.purpose: Shows the mathematical foundation: how the basis functions mathematically blend the Hermite parameters to produce the curve
